item.title: Starting point
item.description:
The start of my journey in deep learning

These are some of my learning today.

  1. Commit to finishing the course.

  2. Finish a project. This can get your foot in interesting places and get you exciting opportunities. The project doesn’t have to be world changing and novel. You can start small and improve overtime.

  3. Be tenacious when learning and building useful stuff. Don’t let obstacles stop you.

  4. Stop keeping preparing.

  5. Have a tinkering mindset that wants to play around with tech and try new useful ideas.

  6. Share your work no matter how insignificant it may seem to you.

  7. Do this in order:

    1. Watch the lecture

    2. Run notebook & experiment

    3. Reproduce results

    4. Repeat with different dataset

  8. You don’t need a lot of match to do useful stuff and train models. There is a lot of gate keeping. Don’t let that stop you.

  9. Start blogging. You can turn videos and lectures you liked into a blog. It may reach a new audience and get a lot more attention than the original source. This could be a practice for your writing skills.

  10. Writing machine learning code that works is hard. Try to build the simplest solution that works end-to-end first and then improve it incrementally afterwards.

  11. Your portfolio is your footprint so build it early.

  12. Start participating in Kaggle competitions early because they will enable you to go through the cycle and it is closer to the real world.
